 {"id":"bd-1dnn","title":"Add wa robot why command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot why <code>` command for agents to understand error codes and policy denials.

## Rationale
When agents receive errors like `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`, they need programmatic access to explanations and remediation steps.

## Implementation
1. Add `RobotCommands::Why { code: String }`
2. Query `wa_core::explanations::get_explanation(code)`
3. Return JSON with: scenario, brief, detailed, suggestions, see_also
4. Create wa-robot-why.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "code": "deny.alt_screen",
    "scenario": "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    "brief": "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    "detailed": "The pane is currently displaying...",
    "suggestions": ["Exit the full-screen application first", ...],
    "see_also": ["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot why deny.alt_screen` returns explanation
- `wa robot why unknown.code` returns error with available codes
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.633050084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476889868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476841497Z","close_reason":"Implemented: wa robot why command using get_explanation()","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dnn","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.663015047Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ey4","title":"Add wa robot workflow abort command","description":"# wa robot workflow abort

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-abort.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Implement workflow abort with cleanup and audit trail.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id and optional --reason
2. Validate workflow is abortable (not already completed)
3. Mark as aborting, run cleanup steps
4. Release pane lock
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Abort stops running workflow
- Unit: Cleanup steps run (unless --force)
- Unit: Pane lock released
- Unit: Error code stability (E_ALREADY_COMPLETED, etc.)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safely aborts running workflows
- [ ] Cleanup steps execute (unless --force)
- [ ] Pane lock released after abort
- [ ] Audit trail records abort with reason
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"bd-1zgb","title":"E2E: reliability hardening scenarios with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate resilience features end-to-end with detailed logging and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Execute fault injection scenarios that trigger circuit breaker, retry, and graceful degradation paths.
- Capture verbose logs (trace/debug) plus key metrics snapshots as artifacts.
- Verify user-facing output is stable and actionable during degraded mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scenarios pass locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, triggered fault labels, and observed policy outputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.619323361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826611461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826558241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:44.505086097Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"wa-37x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.648968364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2kl8","title":"Change wa robot workflow to require explicit 'run' subcommand","description":"## Goal
Change `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` to `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` for consistency with planned workflow list/status/abort subcommands.

## Breaking Change
This is a breaking change. Agents using `wa robot workflow` must update to `wa robot workflow run`.

## Implementation
1. Add `WorkflowSubcommand` enum with `Run`, `List`, `Status`, `Abort` variants
2. Change `RobotCommands::Workflow` to take subcommand instead of positional args
3. Update quick-start guide examples
4. Update error messages to reference new syntax

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` works as before
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` shows helpful error pointing to new syntax
- Quick-start guide reflects new command structure","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:08.619598006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068422868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068365820Z","close_reason":"Implemented: workflow run verb change with subcommand pattern","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kl8","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:08.649391385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rwq","title":"Rename --unhandled-only flag to --unhandled","description":"## Goal
Align `wa robot events` flag with human CLI `wa events --unhandled`.

## Implementation
1. Rename `--unhandled-only` to `--unhandled` in RobotCommands::Events
2. Keep `--unhandled-only` as deprecated alias for backwards compatibility
3. Update quick-start guide

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot events --unhandled` works
- `wa robot events --unhandled-only` still works but is deprecated
- No breaking change for existing users","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:12.005890079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:59.365561884Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:59.365509565Z","close_reason":"Implemented: --unhandled flag with visible_alias for backwards compat","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rwq","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:12.035918241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3p04","title":"Add wa robot approve command","description":"# wa robot approve

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-approve.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Integrate with ApprovalStore to validate and consume tokens.

## Implementation
1. Accept approval code parameter
2. Query ApprovalStore for token
3. Validate workspace scope, expiry, fingerprint
4. Mark as consumed on success
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Valid code consumes token and returns success
- Unit: Expired codes return E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED
- Unit: Already-consumed codes return E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED
- Unit: Wrong pane/workspace returns appropriate error
- E2E: Full approval flow (request → grant → consume)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validates approval codes via ApprovalStore
- [ ] Consumes token on success
- [ ] All error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] Audit trail records approval consumption
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

-{"id":"bd-h1ai","title":"EPIC: Robot Mode API Harmonization","description":"# EPIC: Robot Mode API Harmonization

## Goal
Harmonize the wa robot mode API for consistency, ergonomics, and agent-friendliness.

## Status: MOSTLY COMPLETE

### Done (CLI Structure + Full Implementation)
✅ Change `wa robot workflow <name>` → `wa robot workflow run <name>` (bd-2kl8)
✅ Rename `--unhandled-only` → `--unhandled` with backwards-compat alias (bd-2rwq)
✅ Add `wa robot why <code>` command - fully implemented (bd-1dnn)
✅ Create JSON schemas for all new commands (bd-wlqi)
✅ Update quick-start guide with new syntax (bd-ax3e)

### Deferred to Existing Beads
The following commands have CLI stubs; full implementation tracked elsewhere:
- `wa robot workflow list` → use **wa-35l** (more comprehensive)
- `wa robot workflow status` → use **wa-7dd** (more comprehensive)
- `wa robot workflow abort` → use **wa-55y** (more comprehensive)
- `wa robot approve` → use **bd-39wn** (new, detailed spec)

### Testing
- **bd-qvbz**: Robot workflow subcommands unit + E2E tests
- **wa-4vx.7.8**: Robot mode tests (envelope stability)

## Acceptance Criteria
This EPIC is complete when:
- [x] CLI structure harmonized (done)
- [x] JSON schemas created (done)
- [x] Quick-start updated (done)
- [ ] wa-35l, wa-7dd, wa-55y, bd-39wn implementations done
- [ ] bd-qvbz tests pass with detailed logging

## Note on Bead Consolidation
Original child beads bd-3rwt, bd-27f3, bd-1ey4, bd-3p04 were closed as duplicates of more comprehensive wa-* beads. This avoids duplicate work and leverages the better-specified existing beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:20:47.000012947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T19:44:54.069748084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-h1ai","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Child beads reopened - full implementation pending infrastructure dependencies","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:54:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qvbz","title":"Robot workflow subcommands: unit + E2E tests with detailed logging","description":"# Robot workflow subcommands testing

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing for the harmonized workflow subcommand structure:
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane>` (syntax change from bd-2kl8)
- `wa robot workflow list` (implementation in wa-35l)
- `wa robot workflow status <id>` (implementation in wa-7dd)
- `wa robot workflow abort <id>` (implementation in wa-55y)
- `wa robot events --unhandled` (alias from bd-2rwq)

## Unit Tests

### CLI Parsing Tests (Already Implemented)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_run_subcommand_parsing() {
    // Old syntax should fail
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Implicit run should be rejected");
    
    // New syntax should work
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "run", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_unhandled_flag_alias() {
    // Both flags should parse to same value
    let result1 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled"]);
    let result2 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled-only"]);
    assert_eq!(result1.unhandled_only, result2.unhandled_only);
}
```

### JSON Schema Validation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_list_json_schema() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-list.json");
    assert!(output["data"]["workflows"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_status_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-status.json");
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_abort_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-abort.json");
}
```

### Error Code Stability Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "nonexistent"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}
```

## E2E Tests

### Scenario: Full Workflow Lifecycle
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_lifecycle.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="$ARTIFACT_DIR/workflow_lifecycle.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow Lifecycle E2E ==="

# 1. List workflows
log "Listing workflows..."
LIST=$(wa robot workflow list)
log "List: $LIST"
echo "$LIST" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: list"; exit 1; }

# 2. Run a workflow (dry-run)
log "Starting workflow (dry-run)..."
RUN=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction 0 --dry-run || true)
log "Run: $RUN"

# 3. Check status (if execution ID available)
EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')
if [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "Checking status for $EXEC_ID..."
    STATUS=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID")
    log "Status: $STATUS"
fi

log "=== PASS: workflow_lifecycle ==="
```

### Scenario: Events Unhandled Flag
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_events_unhandled.sh
log "Testing --unhandled alias..."
O1=$(wa robot events --unhandled)
O2=$(wa robot events --unhandled-only)
echo "$O1" | jq -e '.ok' && echo "$O2" | jq -e '.ok' || exit 1
log "=== PASS: events_unhandled ==="
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every action
- Log raw JSON output for debugging
- Dump full output + stack trace on failure
- Summary: PASS/FAIL, elapsed_ms, key assertions

## Dependencies
- wa-35l: workflow list implementation
- wa-7dd: workflow status implementation
- wa-55y: workflow abort implementation
- wa-nu4.1.1: workflow engine core

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI parsing tests pass
- [ ] JSON schema validation tests pass
- [ ] Error code stability tests pass
- [ ] E2E workflow lifecycle test passes
- [ ] E2E events unhandled test passes

 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:47.047519125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:47.047453772Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:44.971218440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:19.090737493Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:55:36.964558253Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:55:36.203121200Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-03j","title":"Workflow: handle_claude_code_limits (Claude Code session/token limits)","description":"# Workflow: handle_claude_code_limits

## Purpose
Handle Claude Code specific limit scenarios:
- Session length limits (context window exhaustion)
- Token budget limits (per-session or per-day)
- Rate limiting

## Detection Patterns
```
# Context exhaustion
"I'll need to summarize our conversation"
"context window is getting full"

# Session limits
"session has reached its limit"
"Please start a new conversation"

# Rate limiting
"You've sent too many messages"
"Please wait before sending"
```

## Workflow Steps

### Step 1: Detect and classify limit type
```rust
match detection.extracted["limit_type"] {
    "context" => handle_context_limit(),
    "session" => handle_session_limit(),
    "rate" => handle_rate_limit(),
}
```

### Step 2: Context limit handling
1. Capture current task state from transcript
2. Wait for natural breakpoint (prompt boundary)
3. Suggest `/compact` or manual conversation restart
4. If auto-handling enabled: send `/compact` command
5. After compaction, re-inject critical context (AGENTS.md)

### Step 3: Session limit handling
1. Save session state (transcript, current file, task progress)
2. Notify user of limit reached
3. Provide command to start fresh session with context restore

### Step 4: Rate limit handling
1. Calculate wait time from message
2. Pause workflow execution
3. Resume when rate limit window passes
4. No aggressive retry

## Safety Constraints
- Never auto-restart without user config allowing it
- Preserve user's work (save transcript)
- Respect Claude Code's /compact semantics

## Configuration
```toml
[workflows.handle_claude_code_limits]
auto_compact = false  # Require explicit enable
save_transcript = true
notify_on_limit = true
```

## Testing
- Fixture: context limit detection patterns
- Fixture: session limit detection patterns
- Fixture: rate limit detection patterns
- Unit: workflow step logic
- E2E: limit detection → notification flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Claude Code limit types detected
- [ ] Context limits handled via /compact when enabled
- [ ] Session limits preserve state
- [ ] Rate limits respected (no retry spam)
- [ ] User notified of limits","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:37.390261502Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:46:21.801662860Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-tests","area-ux","area-workflows","phase-3","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:46:21.801575285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:46:19.743581117Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:50.798419112Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:50.386117223Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-086","title":"Observability tests: log format validation, metrics accuracy, health endpoint E2E","description":"# Observability Testing Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive tests for all observability components.

## Test Categories

### 1. Structured Logging Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_json_log_format() {
    let output = capture_logs(|| {
        tracing::info\!(pane_id = 3, "Test message");
    });
    
    let entry: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
    assert_eq\!(entry["level"], "INFO");
    assert_eq\!(entry["pane_id"], 3);
    assert\!(entry["timestamp"].is_string());
}

#[test]
fn test_sensitive_data_redaction() {
    let text = "export API_KEY=sk-1234567890abcdef";
    let redacted = redact_secrets(text);
    assert\!(\!redacted.contains("sk-1234567890"));
    assert\!(redacted.contains("[REDACTED]"));
}

#[test]
fn test_log_level_filtering() {
    init_logging(&LogConfig { log_level: "warn".into(), .. });
    let output = capture_logs(|| {
        tracing::debug\!("Should not appear");
        tracing::warn\!("Should appear");
    });
    assert\!(\!output.contains("Should not appear"));
    assert\!(output.contains("Should appear"));
}
```

### 2. Metrics Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_counter_increments() {
    EVENTS_DETECTED.with_label_values(&["usage_limit", "codex"]).inc();
    EVENTS_DETECTED.with_label_values(&["usage_limit", "codex"]).inc();
    
    let count = EVENTS_DETECTED
        .with_label_values(&["usage_limit", "codex"])
        .get();
    assert_eq\!(count, 2.0);
}

#[test]
fn test_prometheus_format() {
    let encoder = prometheus::TextEncoder::new();
    let families = REGISTRY.gather();
    let mut buffer = Vec::new();
    encoder.encode(&families, &mut buffer).unwrap();
    let output = String::from_utf8(buffer).unwrap();
    
    assert\!(output.contains("wa_events_detected_total"));
    assert\!(output.contains("# HELP"));
    assert\!(output.contains("# TYPE"));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_metrics_endpoint() {
    let app = test_app();
    let response = app.get("/metrics").await;
    
    assert_eq\!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);
    assert\!(response.headers()["content-type"]
        .to_str().unwrap()
        .contains("text/plain"));
}
```

### 3. Health Endpoint Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_liveness_always_ok() {
    let app = test_app();
    let response = app.get("/health/live").await;
    assert_eq\!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_readiness_when_healthy() {
    let app = test_app_with_mocks(healthy_mocks());
    let response = app.get("/health/ready").await;
    assert_eq\!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_readiness_when_unhealthy() {
    let app = test_app_with_mocks(unhealthy_db_mock());
    let response = app.get("/health/ready").await;
    assert_eq\!(response.status(), StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_health_response_structure() {
    let app = test_app();
    let response = app.get("/health").await;
    let body: HealthResponse = response.json().await;
    
    assert\!(body.checks.contains_key("wezterm_connection"));
    assert\!(body.checks.contains_key("database"));
    assert\!(body.uptime_seconds > 0);
}
```

### 4. E2E Observability Tests
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# tests/e2e/test_observability.sh

set -euo pipefail

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*"; }

# Start wa with observability enabled
log "Starting wa with metrics enabled..."
wa watch --metrics-port 9876 &
WA_PID=$\!
trap "kill $WA_PID 2>/dev/null || true" EXIT
sleep 2

# Test metrics endpoint
log "Testing metrics endpoint..."
METRICS=$(curl -s localhost:9876/metrics)
echo "$METRICS" | grep -q "wa_panes_observed" || { log "FAIL: Missing panes metric"; exit 1; }

# Test health endpoint
log "Testing health endpoint..."
HEALTH=$(curl -s localhost:9876/health)
echo "$HEALTH" | jq -e ".status" || { log "FAIL: Invalid health response"; exit 1; }

# Trigger an event and verify metrics increment
log "Triggering event and checking metrics..."
BEFORE=$(curl -s localhost:9876/metrics | grep "wa_events_detected_total" | tail -1 | awk "{print \$NF}")
# ... trigger event ...
AFTER=$(curl -s localhost:9876/metrics | grep "wa_events_detected_total" | tail -1 | awk "{print \$NF}")
# Verify increment

log "All observability E2E tests passed\!"
```

## Test Fixtures
- Sample log entries for format validation
- Mock health check responses
- Prometheus output snapshots

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Log format tests for JSON and pretty modes
- [ ] Redaction tests for all sensitive patterns
- [ ] Metrics increment tests for all counters
- [ ] Health endpoint response tests
- [ ] E2E script validates full observability stack

## Testing
- Unit: structured logging format, redaction, log-level filtering.
- Integration: metrics encoder + /metrics response headers; /health endpoints behavior.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/test_observability.sh` with timestamped logs and artifact capture.
- Logging: all E2E runs must write a log file under `/tmp/wa_e2e_observability_*.log` and print a final summary line with the path.

-{"id":"wa-0go","title":"[EPIC] Natural Language Event Descriptions: human-readable event summaries","description":"# [EPIC] Natural Language Event Descriptions

## Mission
Transform cryptic event types and codes into clear, human-readable descriptions that users immediately understand.

## Why This Matters
Current events are technical:
- `codex.usage_limit_reached` - What does this mean?
- `WA-4040` - What happened?

Natural language transforms:
- "Codex hit your daily usage limit. You can switch accounts or wait until reset."
- "The send was blocked because the pane is in alternate screen mode (vim is running)."

## Scope

### Event Templates
Each event type has a human-readable template:
```rust
pub struct EventTemplate {
    event_type: String,
    summary: String,           // "Codex usage limit reached"
    description: String,       // Full explanation
    context_keys: Vec<String>, // Variables to interpolate
    suggestions: Vec<String>,  // What user can do
}
```

### Template Interpolation
```
Event: codex.usage_limit_reached
Summary: "Codex reached 100% of daily usage limit"
Description: "Your Codex session in pane {pane_title} has consumed 
             all available tokens for today. The session will pause 
             until {reset_time}."
Suggestions:
  - "Switch to a different account with `wa accounts switch`"
  - "Wait for reset at {reset_time}"
  - "Check usage details with `wa analytics`"
```

### Error Code Descriptions
```rust
pub fn describe_error(code: &str) -> String {
    match code {
        "WA-4040" => "The specified pane was not found. It may have been closed.",
        "WA-4030" => "This action was blocked by policy. The pane may be in a 
                     protected state (running command, alternate screen, etc.)",
        // ...
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
- `wa events` shows natural language summaries
- `wa why <error>` explains decisions
- Error messages include descriptions

## Success Criteria
- Users understand events without looking up codes
- Descriptions are actionable
- All events have human-readable descriptions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All event types have templates
- [ ] All error codes have descriptions
- [ ] Interpolation works correctly
- [ ] Suggestions are relevant
- [ ] CLI shows natural language output
- [ ] Tests cover all templates

## Testing
- Unit tests for template coverage, interpolation, and redaction.
- Integration tests for CLI rendering.
- E2E artifacts include rendered summaries.

 {"id":"wa-0go.1","title":"Event description templates + interpolation engine","description":"# Task: Event description templates + interpolation engine

## Goal
Create a **central, versioned registry** of human‑readable descriptions for every event type and error code.

## Scope
- Event templates (summary + description + suggestions)
- Error code descriptions (e.g., WA-4xxx)
- Template interpolation with **strict redaction**
- Stable identifiers for templates so diffs are auditable

## Deliverables
1. **Template registry** (static data + loader):
   - `event_type -> template`
   - `error_code -> description`
2. **Interpolation engine**:
   - required keys validated at runtime
   - missing keys produce structured “incomplete context” errors
   - redaction pass applied before rendering
3. **Schema/format**:
   - docstring or JSON schema for templates
   - version number for template registry

## Safety / Redaction
- Run redaction before interpolation output is surfaced.
- Never emit raw tokens, device codes, or secret‑like strings.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - template coverage: every event_type has a template
  - interpolation: required keys enforced, missing keys yield structured errors
  - redaction: known secret patterns are removed from rendered output

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every event type and error code has a template entry.
- Interpolation failures are explicit and non‑fatal.
- Redaction is applied consistently to all rendered strings.

 {"id":"wa-0go.2","title":"Wire natural-language descriptions into CLI/UX","description":"# Task: Wire natural‑language descriptions into CLI/UX

## Goal
Surface human‑readable summaries everywhere users see events or errors.

## Scope
- `wa events` output includes:
  - summary + description
  - suggested next steps
- Error surfaces include descriptions:
  - `wa send`/`wa workflow`/`wa approve` failures
  - `wa why` output (if available) includes event templates
- Robot mode **unchanged** (machine output stays structured), but may include a `human_summary` field when `--human` is explicitly requested.

## Deliverables
- CLI rendering logic that attaches templates to event records
- Consistent formatting (TTY + non‑TTY JSON)
- Optional flag `--no-summary` for terse output

## Testing
- Snapshot tests for `wa events` showing:
  - summary lines
  - rendered suggestions
- Error snapshot tests: human‑readable message included

## Acceptance Criteria
- Human CLI shows natural language for every event/error.
- Non‑TTY output includes description fields in JSON.
- No regressions to robot output schema.

 {"id":"wa-0go.3","title":"Tests/E2E — natural-language event descriptions","description":"# Task: Tests/E2E — natural‑language event descriptions

## Goal
Validate template coverage, redaction, and UX rendering with **actionable logging**.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - template completeness (all event types + error codes covered)
  - interpolation correctness with missing/extra keys
  - redaction removes tokens/codes from rendered output
- Integration tests:
  - `wa events` JSON includes summary/description fields
  - TTY output includes summaries and suggestions in stable order

- E2E extension (verbose artifacts):
  - Extend existing events E2E (`wa-4vx.10.13` or `wa-4vx.10.7`) to assert
    natural‑language fields appear for a known event.
  - Capture:
    - raw event JSON
    - rendered TTY snapshot
    - logs with template version and interpolation keys

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if any event type lacks a template.
- E2E artifacts clearly show the rendered description for at least one event.
